rulings.Case Title: STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN ET AL VS. SALMAN SHARIAT
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING RE: PETITIONERS STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION TO CONFIRM ORDER OF SAN FRANCISCO RENT BOARD.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2024 line 1. HEARING RE: PETITIONERS STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION TO CONFIRM ORDER OF SAN FRANCISCO RENT BOARD. is OFF CALENDAR. This hearing is set on a petition that is no longer the operative pleading in this action. It appears from the register of actions that an Amended Petition was filed after respondent's opposition and then appears to have been supplemented 5 days later by a purported Exhibit to the Petition. Petitioner may set the hearing on this petition when the Petition is complete in its final form and accompanied by any and all supporting documents (Points and Authorities, Declaration, Request for Judicial Notice, etc) submitted for filing on the same date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HIU TUNG YIP VS. 655 POWELL I5, LP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANTS 655 POWELL I5, LP AND GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry their burden and show that the negligence claim arises out of protected activity. No authority is cited for the propositions that "removal" or a single tenant by a private landlord is an issue of public interest or that "the right to not file a petition" is a protected activity under CCP 425.16. The court does not find this motion frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
